mnemonics: ANOVA Compares Means (AOV: Analysis Of Variance)
SS Stands for Sum of Squares (SS: Sum of Squared deviations)
F-Ratio is For Finding significance (F-test for Factor effect)
keyPoints: ANOVA tests for differences among group means by analyzing variance components.
The F-statistic compares the variance between groups to the variance within groups.
Rejecting the null hypothesis (H₀) indicates at least one group mean is significantly different.
commonMistakes: Assuming ANOVA identifies which specific groups differ (requires post-hoc tests).
Failing to check assumptions like normality and homogeneity of variances before running ANOVA.
